$comment: Single source of truth for NemoClaw launch claims and platform support. Covers platforms, inference providers, supported agents, messaging integrations, and deployment paths. Scripts read this to generate README and docs tables. QA/CI update platform/provider rows; the engineering owner reviews other rows. Docs are derived.

project_status.label: Early preview
project_status.notes: Maintainers review issues, discussions, and PRs on a best-effort basis without guaranteed response timelines.

owners.$comment: Engineering owner is the GitHub team that auto-reviews changes to this file via CODEOWNERS and signs off on launch-facing claim changes before they reach demos or sales material. NemoClaw is maintainer-run; there is no separate product owner role today.

statuses.unsupported: Explicitly out of scope. Not validated and not planned.
statuses.hermes only: Available only when onboarding the Hermes agent.

platforms.name: Linux
platforms.notes: Primary tested path. Ubuntu 24.04 is the validated distro in production source (`DEFAULT_COMPAT_IMAGE` in `src/lib/onboard/docker-driver-gateway-launch.ts:10` and the preflight tests pin 24.04 only); the installer's package-manager probes assume apt-get. Other distros (Ubuntu 22.04, Fedora, Rocky, Alma, NixOS, Arch) may work but are not validated.

platforms.name: macOS (Apple Silicon)
platforms.notes: Start the container runtime (Colima or Docker Desktop) before running the installer. Xcode Command Line Tools (`xcode-select --install`) are typically required for Node native modules during install â€” recommended, not preflight-enforced by NemoClaw.

platforms.name: DGX Spark
platforms.notes: Use the standard installer and `$$nemoclaw onboard`. For an end-to-end walkthrough with local Ollama inference, see the [NVIDIA Spark playbook](https://build.nvidia.com/spark/nemoclaw).

platforms.name: Windows WSL2
platforms._prd_note: PRD tracks x86 and ARM (WOA) separately; treating as one entry until ARM is validated independently.
platforms.notes: Requires WSL2 with Docker Desktop backend.

platforms.name: DGX Station
platforms.notes: P1 per PRD. Workstation form-factor with NVIDIA GPUs and the same Docker + NVIDIA Container Toolkit + CDI requirements as DGX Spark. Onboard path not yet validated end-to-end on the hardware; vLLM has a placeholder default model defined for this host class (`Qwen/Qwen3.6-27B-FP8`) that will move out of `deferred` once the hardware run is signed off.

platforms.name: NVIDIA RTX (consumer and Pro workstation GPUs)
platforms.notes: P1 per PRD. Covers RTX consumer cards and RTX Pro workstation cards on Linux hosts that meet the generic-Linux-GPU requirements (NVIDIA Container Toolkit + CDI present). The provider menu emits managed vLLM behind `NEMOCLAW_EXPERIMENTAL=1` or `NEMOCLAW_PROVIDER=install-vllm` for this host class today; the end-to-end onboard path on this hardware is not yet validated in CI.

providers.name: Other OpenAI-compatible endpoint
providers.notes: Adapter path validated with OpenRouter (`https://openrouter.ai`, see `src/lib/onboard.ts:3673`). Behavior on other OpenAI-compatible proxies, gateways, and self-hosted implementations may vary; this row claims the adapter, not the universe of compatible endpoints.

providers.name: Other Anthropic-compatible endpoint
providers.notes: Adapter path validated with AWS Bedrock (`src/lib/onboard/bedrock-runtime.ts`). Behavior on other Anthropic-compatible proxies and gateways may vary; this row claims the adapter, not the universe of compatible endpoints.

providers.name: Hermes Provider
providers.notes: Available when onboarding Hermes Agent through `nemohermes`

providers.name: Local Ollama
providers.notes: Available when Ollama is installed or running on the host. Validated default models: `qwen3.6:35b` (high VRAM), `nemotron-3-nano:30b` (medium VRAM), `qwen3.5:9b` (low VRAM fallback).

providers.name: Local NVIDIA NIM
providers.notes: Requires `NEMOCLAW_EXPERIMENTAL=1` and a NIM-capable NVIDIA GPU. Host must have the NVIDIA Container Toolkit installed and a CDI spec present (`onboard` asserts CDI presence via `assertCdiNvidiaGpuSpecPresent`, `src/lib/onboard.ts:1581`). NIM images pull from `nvcr.io` and require NGC registry login. Gated behind the experimental flag because NemoClaw does not auto-select a NIM image for the host today â€” the user must explicitly pick from the validated image list (managed vLLM has per-host default models and so is not gated on the same boxes). Validated images referenced in `src/lib/inference/config.ts` and `nemoclaw/src/index.ts`: `nvidia/nemotron-3-super-120b-a12b` (default cloud model), `nvidia/nemotron-3-nano-30b-a3b`, `nvidia/llama-3.3-nemotron-super-49b-v1.5`.

providers.name: Local vLLM (already running)
providers.notes: Appears in the onboarding menu when NemoClaw detects a server already on `localhost:8000`. No flag required. Model is whatever the existing server serves.

providers.name: Local vLLM (managed install/start)
providers.notes: Appears by default on DGX Spark and DGX Station. Generic Linux NVIDIA GPU hosts require `NEMOCLAW_EXPERIMENTAL=1` or `NEMOCLAW_PROVIDER=install-vllm`. Host must have the NVIDIA Container Toolkit installed and a CDI spec present (`onboard` asserts CDI presence). NemoClaw pulls/starts the stable NGC vLLM container per host profile (see `src/lib/inference/vllm.ts:55,177` for the pins): DGX Spark / DGX Station â†’ `nvcr.io/nvidia/vllm:26.05.post1-py3`; generic Linux NVIDIA GPU â†’ `nvcr.io/nvidia/vllm:26.03.post1-py3`. Validated defaults (`src/lib/inference/vllm-models.ts`): DGX Spark â†’ `nvidia/Qwen3.6-35B-A3B-NVFP4`; DGX Station â†’ `Qwen/Qwen3.6-27B-FP8`; Linux NVIDIA GPU â†’ `nvidia/NVIDIA-Nemotron-3-Nano-4B-FP8`. Image pulls require NGC registry login (`docker login nvcr.io`); onboard prompts for the NGC API key when authentication is missing.

agents.name: OpenClaw
agents.notes: Default agent runtime. Onboard with `$$nemoclaw onboard` (no `--agent` flag required).

agents.name: Hermes
agents.notes: First-class agent with dedicated CLI (`$$nemohermes`), Dockerfile, manifest, docs, and nightly E2E job coverage (`hermes-e2e` job in `.github/workflows/nightly-e2e.yaml` plus the `hermes-*-vitest` jobs in `e2e-vitest-scenarios.yaml`). Onboard with `$$nemohermes onboard` or pass `--agent hermes` to `$$nemoclaw onboard`. Unlocks the Hermes Provider inference route. Known structural gaps: model-provider compatibility registry is empty (backfilled per failure, see `nemoclaw-blueprint/model-specific-setup/hermes/README.md`); no Hermes-specific unit tests in `nemoclaw/src/`; macOS and WSL CI suites do not differentiate agents. Suitable for evaluation and the documented onboarding paths; production parity with OpenClaw is not yet asserted.

capabilities.name: Guided onboarding
capabilities.notes: Single-command interactive wizard (`$$nemoclaw onboard`) that walks the user through inference provider selection, credential setup, sandbox creation, and dashboard launch. Non-interactive mode is supported via `--non-interactive` + `NEMOCLAW_*` environment variables for CI and scripted installs.

capabilities.name: Sandboxed execution
capabilities.notes: Landlock, seccomp, network namespace isolation, no-new-privileges, privilege dropping, and process limits (ulimit -u 512 at `scripts/lib/sandbox-init.sh:237`). The `DANGEROUS_CAPS` list at `scripts/lib/sandbox-init.sh:288-299` drops cap_sys_admin, cap_sys_ptrace, cap_net_raw, cap_dac_override, cap_sys_chroot, cap_fsetid, cap_setfcap, cap_mknod, cap_audit_write, cap_net_bind_service via `capsh --drop` when CAP_SETPCAP is present. The fail-closed bounding-set gate is opt-in via `NEMOCLAW_REQUIRE_CAP_DROP=1` (default is warn-and-continue so hosts without CAP_SETPCAP still boot); see #3280.

capabilities.name: Routed inference
capabilities.notes: Provider-routed model calls through the OpenShell gateway, transparent to the agent. The agent uses `inference.local` inside the sandbox; provider credentials stay on the host. Supports every entry in the Providers table.

capabilities.name: Declarative network policy
capabilities.notes: YAML-defined egress with policy presets. Presets include `slack`, `discord`, `telegram`, `weather`, `openclaw-pricing`, `huggingface`, `npm`, `pypi`, `brew`, and others. Hot-reloadable at runtime via `$$nemoclaw <name> policy-add`.

capabilities.name: Snapshot and restore
capabilities.notes: Create, list, and restore named snapshots of sandbox state via the `$$nemoclaw <name> snapshot` subcommands (`create`, `list`, `restore`). Credential stripping is enforced on capture. Unsafe symlinks are rejected on restore.

capabilities.name: Agent skills
capabilities.notes: Packaged agent skills are discoverable by Cursor, Claude Code, and other coding assistants via `.agents/skills/`. Skills also install into the sandbox via `$$nemoclaw <name> skill install`.

capabilities.name: State migration
capabilities.notes: Sandbox state migrates across rebuilds with credentials intentionally excluded. Hermes excludes `auth.json` and restores its SQLite session DB through the backup API. OpenClaw config merge prevents stale state from overwriting fresh values.

capabilities.name: Blueprint versioning
capabilities.notes: Versioned, digest-verified, and reproducible blueprint lifecycle. Drives `$$nemoclaw <name> rebuild` and the migration safeguards above.

capabilities.name: Web search backend
capabilities.notes: Runtime-configurable web-search backend plumbed through the OpenShell gateway. Brave is the currently-implemented backend (see `src/lib/onboard/brave-provider-profile.ts` and `src/lib/onboard/web-search-flow.ts`); backend credentials are user-supplied via an onboard prompt â€” no NemoClaw-bundled key.

out_of_scope.name: Podman / other container runtimes
out_of_scope.notes: Onboard surfaces an explicit unsupported-runtime error for Podman (`src/lib/onboard.ts:1611` prints the rejection; `src/lib/onboard/preflight.ts:586` flags the unsupported runtime upstream). Only Docker Engine, Docker Desktop, and Colima are supported. See issue #420 (closed).

out_of_scope.name: Intel Mac (macOS x86_64)
out_of_scope.notes: OpenShell does not publish macOS x86_64 standalone gateway assets. Install hard-fails on x86_64 macOS (`scripts/install-openshell.sh:315`). See issue #954 (closed).

out_of_scope.name: Non-Ubuntu/Debian Linux distros
out_of_scope.notes: Installer assumes `apt-get`. Fedora/Rocky/Alma/Arch/NixOS are not validated and the installer's package-manager probes do not cover them. See open issue #899 (Fedora hang).

out_of_scope.name: Native Kubernetes or OpenShift deployments
out_of_scope.notes: NemoClaw runs the sandbox as a Docker container, not a Kubernetes pod. The default Docker-driver topology does not embed k3s. Operator-managed K8s/OpenShift deployments are out of scope; see issue #407 (community OpenShift via agent-sandbox CRD).

out_of_scope.name: Air-gapped / offline installs
out_of_scope.notes: Onboard assumes network reachability for package fetches, container pulls, and provider validation. See open issues #4872 and #2218 (production-deployment epic covering air-gapped support, China network guidance, multi-host topology).

out_of_scope.name: Windows-on-ARM GPU passthrough
out_of_scope.notes: Windows-on-ARM CPU paths run under WSL2 'tested with limitations', but GPU passthrough on WOA is denylisted (`src/lib/onboard/wsl-docker-desktop-gpu.ts:188`, `src/lib/inference/gpu-trust.test.ts:70`). See closed issue #4565.

out_of_scope.name: Non-NVIDIA GPUs (AMD/ROCm, Intel Arc, Apple Metal)
out_of_scope.notes: Local vLLM and NIM paths assert NVIDIA CDI presence via `assertCdiNvidiaGpuSpecPresent` (`src/lib/onboard.ts:1581`, called at `:1646`). NemoClaw does not install non-NVIDIA accelerator drivers.

out_of_scope.name: LangChain, AutoGen, CrewAI, or other agent harnesses
out_of_scope.notes: Only OpenClaw and Hermes are integrated. Bringing other harnesses is tracked as a research epic (see open issue #4861) but is not on the current roadmap.

out_of_scope.name: Multi-user host sharing
out_of_scope.notes: Sandboxes are scoped to a single host user. NemoClaw treats multi-user hosts as a risk and warns at onboard; see `docs/security/openclaw-controls.mdx` Multi-user detection.

out_of_scope.name: Hosted SaaS / managed NemoClaw
out_of_scope.notes: There is no managed offering. Supported deployment paths are Local CLI onboard, Remote GPU via Brev CLI, and Brev web UI.

out_of_scope.name: Native provider integrations not in the Providers table
out_of_scope.notes: Vertex AI, Azure OpenAI, SageMaker, Together.ai, Replicate, and HuggingFace Inference Endpoints are not first-class onboarding entries. AWS Bedrock works through the `compatible-anthropic-endpoint` adapter (`src/lib/onboard/bedrock-runtime.ts`).

out_of_scope.name: Production SLA or guaranteed response times
out_of_scope.notes: NemoClaw is an early-preview alpha project. Maintainers respond on a best-effort basis. No SLA is offered.

integrations.name: Slack
integrations.notes: Configured via OpenShell-managed channel during onboarding. Sandbox egress allowed by the `slack` policy preset.

integrations.name: Discord
integrations.notes: Configured via OpenShell-managed channel during onboarding. Sandbox egress allowed by the `discord` policy preset.

integrations.name: Telegram
integrations.notes: Configured via OpenShell-managed channel during onboarding.

integrations.name: WeChat
integrations.notes: Channel hook available. Verify regional account access before relying on this path.

integrations.name: WhatsApp
integrations.notes: Channel hook available. Verify Meta Business API access before relying on this path.

deployment_paths.name: Local CLI onboard
deployment_paths.notes: Run `$$nemoclaw onboard` on a tested platform with Docker available locally. Primary path.

deployment_paths.name: Remote GPU via Brev CLI
deployment_paths.notes: Legacy compatibility wrapper provisions a Brev VM, installs Docker + NVIDIA Container Toolkit, and runs `$$nemoclaw onboard` on that host. Defaults to GCP; override with `NEMOCLAW_BREV_PROVIDER`. The preferred path is the standard installer followed by `$$nemoclaw onboard` after the VM is reachable.

deployment_paths.name: Brev web UI
deployment_paths.notes: Browser-driven launcher provisions a Brev-managed Linux VM with Docker, the OpenShell runtime, a NemoClaw sandbox running OpenClaw, inference routing, and the OpenClaw dashboard.
